document.title: FileTalk Silk Road Architecture
document.purpose: Describe the architectural insights and patterns underlying the Silk Road (WayStation + Courier) communication model within FileTalk.

core_insight.statement: Programs communicate indirectly through persistent JSON artifacts placed in the filesystem, with delivery achieved by roaming agents (couriers) rather than direct addressing.

contrast.traditional_systems: Programs communicate via direct channels (TCP, HTTP, IPC) requiring known endpoints and active connectivity.
contrast.filetalk_silkroad: Programs emit messages into a shared environment; discovery and delivery occur asynchronously through traversal and interpretation.

filetalk.definition: Use of files (JSON) as the primary medium for program-to-program communication.

message.definition: A JSON artifact representing intent or data to be delivered.
properties: addressed by semantic intent rather than fixed location

waystation.definition: A filesystem directory recognized as a resting and exchange point for messages and directives.

courier.definition: A roaming process that traverses the filesystem, discovers WayStations, interprets directives, and transports messages.

waystation_structure.description: Canonical directory layout for a WayStation.

elements.messages/: Holds message artifacts awaiting pickup or delivery.
elements.directives/: Contains user or system-authored instructions influencing courier behavior.
elements.cache/: Stores non-authoritative knowledge discovered by couriers.
elements.logs/: Optional record of courier activity and message movement.

directives_pattern.definition: Local JSON artifacts that express intent to influence courier behavior.
examples: suggest exploration of a new path

routing_model.description: Messages are not routed by fixed paths but by discovery and interpretation.
properties: delivery may occur via multiple hops
routing_model.implication: Routing is an emergent property of courier behavior rather than a predefined map.

identity_model.description: Soft identity based on names, tags, or capabilities rather than fixed locations.

temporal_properties.description: System behavior is shaped by time rather than immediacy.
properties: no requirement for simultaneous program availability
time becomes part of the routing mechanism

resilience_characteristics.mechanism: Decoupling of message creation, transport, and delivery.

human_interaction_model.description: Humans participate directly by editing files within WayStations.
capabilities: inject directives using simple JSON edits
debug by observation rather than instrumentation
human_interaction_model.principle: The system remains legible and editable without specialized tools.

design_principles: Prefer declarative intent over imperative control
Keep infrastructure passive; place intelligence in agents
Allow multiple interpretations rather than enforcing a single truth
open_questions: How should couriers prioritize competing directives?
What minimal conventions are required for interoperability?
How to prevent message duplication or infinite circulation?
What policies govern message expiration or garbage collection?
How to represent trust or permission in a decentralized filesystem?
